name: Tuwo Masara
intro: This swallow is made from coarsely ground durum wheat. In Nigeria, it is popularly referred to as Semovita, which is a brand of semolina flour. It has a pale, oﬀ-white colour when cooked.

ingredients.item: and 1/2 cups water (2 cups cold; 1/2 cup hot)

method: Pour 2 cups of cold water into a pot and add the semolina. Stir quickly with a wooden spoon and keep mixing over medium-high heat until a sticky dough forms. Add half a cup of hot water and do not stir. Cover and simmer for 6-7 minutes over low heat. Mix vigorously with a wooden spoon until you have a smooth, stretchy dough.

Pour 1 cup of cold water into a pot, add the cornmeal and stir continuously over medium heat. Add 1 cup of hot water and do not stir. Cover and simmer over low heat for 5 minutes. Use a wooden spoon to knead the mixture for 10-15 minutes until you have a smooth, stretchy dough. Leave to cool slightly and it will continue to thicken.
